services.title: React Native Development Services from WiseOSC

items.title: Cross-Platform iOS & Android Apps
paragraphs: WiseOSC builds production React Native applications that share business logic between iOS and Android while still respecting platform conventions—navigation patterns, safe areas, permissions, and store guidelines.

From greenfield apps to scaling existing codebases, WiseOSC structures screens, navigation (React Navigation / Expo Router), and feature modules so teams can parallelize work without constant merge pain.

items.title: Expo & Managed Workflows
paragraphs: When Expo fits your product, WiseOSC leverages EAS Build, EAS Update for over-the-air fixes, and a streamlined dev client workflow. WiseOSC only ejects or adds custom native code when you need capabilities beyond the managed API surface.
Expo accelerates iteration for many teams; WiseOSC still hardens releases with staged rollouts, crash reporting, and clear versioning so OTA updates never surprise users.

items.title: Native Modules & Bridge Integration
paragraphs: Some features require Kotlin/Swift: biometrics, Bluetooth, AR, or proprietary SDKs. WiseOSC implements native modules with clear JS contracts, error handling, and threading considerations so the bridge stays stable under load.
WiseOSC documents integration steps for your in-house native developers and keeps upgrade paths aligned with React Native releases.

items.title: Performance: Lists, Animations & Startup Time
paragraphs: Mobile users feel jank instantly. WiseOSC optimizes FlatList/SectionList patterns, memoization, image sizing, and Hermes-friendly bundles. Animations use Reanimated where appropriate for 60fps interactions.
Startup time matters for retention: WiseOSC analyzes cold start, defers non-critical work, and trims unnecessary dependencies so your app feels snappy on mid-range devices—not only flagships.

items.title: Offline, Sync & Push Notifications
paragraphs: WiseOSC designs offline-first or optimistic UIs when connectivity is unreliable, with clear conflict resolution and background sync policies. Push notification flows integrate with FCM/APNs and respect user consent and OS rules.
Deep linking and universal links are wired so marketing and support can drive users to the right screen inside the app.

items.title: App Store Delivery & Maintenance
paragraphs: WiseOSC supports release trains: beta distribution (TestFlight, Play Internal Testing), store listings, screenshots, and compliance questionnaires for permissions and data handling.
Ongoing work includes React Native upgrades, dependency audits, OS compatibility (new iOS/Android behaviors), and monitoring crashes in production with actionable dashboards.

whyChoose.title: Why Choose WiseOSC for React Native Development
whyChoose.imageAlt: React Native mobile developers at WiseOSC

features.title: Mobile-first delivery discipline
features.body: WiseOSC ships apps that pass store review and behave well in the wild: permissions, background modes, memory, and battery are considered alongside feature velocity. React Native is not “web on phone”—WiseOSC treats it as real mobile engineering.

features.title: Works with your release process
features.body: WiseOSC fits your CI/CD (EAS, Fastlane, Bitrise, GitHub Actions), environments, and staged rollouts. Designers and PMs get builds early via TestFlight/Play tracks so feedback lands before users do.

features.title: Engineers who know the RN stack
features.body: WiseOSC's React Native developers understand navigation, native builds, Hermes, and platform quirks. They collaborate with backend teams on APIs and with native teams when bridges are required.

keyThings.title: Key Things to Know About React Native

sections.heading: 1. One codebase, two stores
sections.body: Shared JavaScript/TypeScript logic and often most UI code reduces duplication versus fully native teams—especially when product iteration speed is critical. Platform-specific tweaks still happen where UX demands it.

sections.heading: 2. React skills transfer
sections.body: Teams already strong in React web can onboard to React Native faster than an entirely new mobile stack—though mobile concerns (navigation, lists, native builds) still require dedicated attention.

sections.heading: 3. Mature ecosystem
sections.body: Libraries exist for common mobile needs: maps, camera, biometrics, notifications. Choosing maintained dependencies and upgrade paths is essential as OS versions evolve.

tabs.label: What is React Native used for?

sections.heading: Consumer & B2B mobile apps
sections.body: From consumer subscriptions to field-service tools, React Native fits many product shapes. Fit depends on UI complexity, animation needs, and how often you must dip into fully native code.

sections.heading: Brownfield & existing apps
sections.body: React Native can be adopted incrementally inside existing native apps (brownfield) or as greenfield. WiseOSC helps assess integration cost and team skills before committing.

sections.heading: Not identical to React web
sections.body: Layout uses Yoga (flexbox), not the DOM. Styling and gestures differ; performance tuning is mobile-specific. Treating RN like responsive web leads to painful rewrites.

sections.heading: Upgrade cadence
sections.body: React Native and Xcode/Android toolchain versions move quickly. A maintenance plan for yearly OS updates and RN releases avoids painful big jumps.

items.question: React Native vs fully native apps—how do we choose?
items.answer: React Native suits many products that need faster iteration and shared logic across iOS and Android. Choose fully native when you need cutting-edge platform APIs, extreme graphics, or a team already 100% invested in Swift/Kotlin only.

items.question: Should we use Expo?
items.answer: Expo is excellent for many teams: faster env setup, OTA updates, and managed builds. If you need custom native code not covered by Expo modules, WiseOSC evaluates prebuild workflow or a bare workflow with clear trade-offs.

items.question: How do you ensure app performance on low-end devices?
items.answer: WiseOSC profiles on real devices, optimizes lists and images, uses Hermes, and avoids unnecessary re-renders. Performance budgets and crash-free session targets are tracked release over release.

items.question: Can React Native apps match native look and feel?
items.answer: Yes, when designers and engineers align on platform guidelines. WiseOSC uses platform-specific components where it matters and tests on both iOS and Android—not only simulators.

items.question: How do updates and app store releases work?
items.answer: JS-level fixes can ship via OTA (where policy allows) with EAS Update; native changes require store builds. WiseOSC coordinates versioning, release notes, and rollback plans.

items.question: What maintenance is required after launch?
items.answer: Expect OS updates, dependency upgrades, React Native version bumps, SDK updates from Apple/Google, and monitoring crashes. A quarterly health review keeps technical debt visible and manageable.
